# token-reduce

> Reduce token burn in Claude Code and Codex by 60-90%. Routes discovery through BM25 path helpers (QMD), compresses command output (RTK), and supports optional companions (AXI, caveman, context-mode, code-review-graph).

## Install (run this once, then restart Claude Code)

```bash
git clone https://github.com/chimera-defi/token-reduce-skill tools/token-reduce-skill
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/setup.sh
```

`setup.sh` installs QMD and RTK, installs AXI companion CLIs (`gh-axi`, `chrome-devtools-axi`) when `npm` is available, wires hook layers into Claude Code globally, and indexes your repo. Safe to re-run.

One-command activation for the measured stack (core-only default):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/chimera-defi/token-reduce-skill tools/token-reduce-skill
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh activate-stack
```

To include conditional companions during activation:

```bash
TOKEN_REDUCE_ACTIVATE_EXTENDED_STACK=1 ./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh activate-stack
```

Optional extended companion install:

```bash
TOKEN_REDUCE_INSTALL_EXTENDED_STACK=1 ./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/setup.sh
```

This attempts to install `context-mode` and `code-review-graph` only when explicitly requested.

Claude Code plugin alternative:

```
claude plugin marketplace add chimera-defi/token-reduce-skill
claude plugin install token-reduce@chimera-defi
```

## Codex new-context handoff

Print a ready-to-paste handoff block for a fresh Codex context:

```bash
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh handoff-codex
```

Static reference template: `references/codex-handoff.md`

## How to use (for agents)

Before reading any file whose path you don't already know, run one of:

```bash
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-adaptive.sh <topic words>
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-paths.sh <topic words>
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-snippet.sh <topic words>
```

`token-reduce-adaptive.sh` auto-promotes/demotes tiers based on query intent and recent helper behavior.

`token-reduce-paths.sh` returns candidate file paths via BM25. Use those paths for targeted `Read` or `Grep` calls — never broad glob or `find .` scans.

`token-reduce-snippet.sh` returns paths plus one ranked excerpt when you need more context to pick the right file.

Do **not** replace the first helper call with raw `rg --files`, raw repo-wide `rg -n`, `find .`, or `grep -R` just because they feel narrow enough. The helper is the first compliant move whenever the path is not already known.

## Routing profiles

```bash
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh settings profile list
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh settings profile apply minimal-load
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh settings profile apply balanced
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh settings profile apply max-savings
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh benchmark-profiles
```

## Decision flow

```
Know the exact file path? → Read it directly
Don't know where it lives? → token-reduce-paths.sh <topic>
Need a snippet too?        → token-reduce-snippet.sh <topic>
After paths returned?      → Grep or Read on exact paths only
```

## What the hooks enforce

Once installed, the hooks enforce this automatically:

- `enforce-token-reduce-first.py` (PreToolUse) — blocks broad Bash scans (`find .`, `ls -R`, `rg --files`), broad Glob patterns, and directory-wide Grep until a path helper has run
- `remind-token-reduce.py` (UserPromptSubmit) — injects a reminder on discovery-shaped prompts

If you see "Blocked broad exploratory Bash scan", run `token-reduce-paths.sh` first.

## Savings benchmark

Numbers from `references/benchmarks/local-benchmark.json` (this repo, QMD warm):

| Approach | Tokens | vs broad inventory |
|----------|--------|-------------------|
| `rg --files .` broad inventory | `1826` | baseline |
| `rg -n -i -g '*.md' "topic" .` scoped | `391` | `78.6%` saved |
| `qmd search` (file-list only) | `309` | `83.1%` saved |
| `token-reduce-paths.sh` (QMD warm) | `31` | `98.3%` saved |
| `token-reduce-snippet.sh` (QMD warm) | `195` | `89.3%` saved |

Full composite-stack benchmark (vs broad shell): `86.4%` saved, quality-pass.
See `references/benchmarks/composite-benchmark.json` for per-strategy detail.

## Optional companion (extra output/input compression)

If installed, `caveman` can be layered on top of token-reduce:

- output compression: `/caveman lite` (or `$caveman lite`)
- memory-file compression: `/caveman:compress CLAUDE.md`

Use this as a companion only. `token-reduce` still owns discovery routing and hook enforcement.

## Optional companion (GitHub/browser execution)

If installed, AXI tools can reduce turns for interface-heavy tasks:

- `gh-axi` for GitHub tasks
- `chrome-devtools-axi` for browser tasks

Use AXI for execution surfaces only. Keep token-reduce as the first move when file location is unknown.

## Managed Harness Controls (optional)

Enable opt-in telemetry summaries and update checks:

```bash
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh settings set telemetry.enabled true
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh settings set telemetry.endpoint https://your-endpoint.example/ingest
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh telemetry-sync
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh updates
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh auto-update
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh release-gate
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh deps-check
./tools/token-reduce-skill/scripts/token-reduce-manage.sh deps-check-conditional
```

## MCP tools (if using MCP)

| Tool | What it does |
|------|-------------|
| `token_reduce_paths` | BM25 path list for a query |
| `token_reduce_snippet` | Paths + one ranked excerpt |
| `token_reduce_setup` | Run full setup (QMD + RTK + hooks) |
| `token_reduce_benchmark` | Run local benchmark |
| `token_reduce_install_info` | Print install commands |

MCP config:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "token-reduce-mcp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["/path/to/token-reduce-skill/mcp/server.mjs"]
    }
  }
}
```
